title: How to Invest in Gold — ETF vs SGB vs Physical
meta_description: Compare Gold ETF, Sovereign Gold Bond, digital gold, and physical gold. Learn which is the best way to invest in gold in India.

hero.one_liner: Gold is a proven hedge against inflation — but buying jewellery isn't investing. Here are smarter ways to own gold.

sections.heading: Why Invest in Gold?
content: Gold has been a trusted store of value for centuries. In India specifically, gold prices have given about 10-11% annual returns over the last 20 years. Gold works as a hedge — when stocks crash, gold often rises, balancing your portfolio.

But buying physical gold (jewellery or coins) comes with making charges (10-25%), purity concerns, storage risk, and no interest income. Modern investment options like Sovereign Gold Bonds, Gold ETFs, and digital gold solve all these problems.

sections.heading: Gold Investment Options Compared
rows: Exit after 5th year / sell on exchange
sections.footnote: SGB is the best option for long-term gold investment due to additional 2.5% interest and zero tax at maturity.

rows.label: Long-term (5+ years)
rows.value: Sovereign Gold Bond — best returns (gold price + 2.5% interest + tax-free at maturity)

rows.label: Short-term trading
rows.value: Gold ETF — buy/sell instantly on stock exchange via Demat account

rows.label: Very small amounts
rows.value: Digital Gold on Paytm/PhonePe — start from ₹1, but no interest and storage fees after time

rows.label: Wearing/gifting
rows.value: Physical gold — only option if you want jewellery, but not ideal as investment

faqs.question: How to buy Sovereign Gold Bond?
faqs.answer: SGBs are issued by RBI in series throughout the year. Buy through your bank's net banking, post office, stock exchange, or apps like Groww and Zerodha. You get ₹50/gram discount for online purchase.

faqs.question: Is gold a good investment in 2026?
faqs.answer: Gold is a portfolio diversifier, not a primary investment. Financial advisors typically recommend allocating 5-10% of your portfolio to gold. It protects against inflation and stock market crashes, but shouldn't be your only investment.

faqs.question: What is the tax on gold investments?
faqs.answer: SGB held to 8-year maturity: completely tax-free. Gold ETF/digital gold sold after 1 year: 12.5% LTCG. Physical gold sold after 2 years: 12.5% LTCG. Sold before these periods: taxed at your income slab rate.

faqs.question: Can I convert digital gold to physical gold?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most digital gold platforms (Augmont, SafeGold via Paytm/PhonePe) allow you to convert your digital gold into physical coins or bars and get them delivered. Minimum is usually 0.5-1 gram.

disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. Gold prices fluctuate based on global factors. Past returns do not guarantee future performance. Consult a financial advisor before investing.
